mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, discount-rate pressure on equities eases and the S&P 500 should rise over the next month. The signal is confirmed if downward rate changes are followed by positive 20-day returns and rejected if easing episodes do not outperform unchanged-rate periods.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of FEDFUNDS-down months, or if the conditional mean return during easing months is not above the unconditional mean over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening labor income and demand, which should pressure corporate revenues and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is falsified if unemployment spikes are followed by consistently positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below zero over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST signals stronger household and construction demand, which should support cyclical earnings expectations and lift the S&P 500 over the next quarter. The signal fails if joint demand strength does not outperform single-series improvement cases.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with both RSAFS and HOUST above their 3-month moving averages do not generate positive 60-day forward returns on average, or if the interaction term is not significant relative to either proxy alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO with stable or weakly rising employment implies manufacturing slowdown without offsetting labor absorption, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if industrial weakness is repeatedly absorbed without equity drawdown.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month average while PAYEMS growth is flat or negative
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than half of observations, or if the combined INDPRO/PAYEMS condition has no better explanatory power than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL inflation relative to the policy rate indicates real-rate compression and a tightening surprise that should lower equity valuations over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS stays below the inflation pace.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by > 2pp
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation-over-policy episodes are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the spread signal is indistinguishable from zero in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS indicates weakening payroll creation and slower income growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through lower expected sales and margins. The hypothesis fails if payroll contractions do not lead to weaker forward equity performance.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average following 3-month PAYEMS declines, or if payroll weakness has no measurable advantage versus random timing over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is accelerating while RSAFS is also expanding, broad final demand is improving enough to raise earnings expectations and support the S&P 500 over the next quarter. The claim is falsified if GDP and retail sales co-acceleration does not align with positive forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth are both above their prior 4-quarter or 3-month averages
failure_condition: Rejected if joint GDPC1/RSAFS acceleration does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered observations, or if the interaction effect is not stable across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing recovery, rising INDPRO combined with improving HOUST signals that production and construction pipelines are strengthening, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as growth-sensitive sectors reprice quickly. The signal is invalid if recovery data do not translate into immediate equity strength.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive month-over-month after a prior decline and HOUST stops falling
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered recovery episodes fail to deliver positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the joint signal loses predictive value once either INDPRO or HOUST is removed.
